title: T-008 — transfer_orders ALTER COLUMN extensions for inter-site (§9.6, D-MS-3)
prompt:
# T-008 — transfer_orders ALTER COLUMN extensions for inter-site

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.6, §9.9 (composite indexes), D-MS-3
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Extend the 05-WH-owned `transfer_orders` table with the 8 inter-site columns + dual cross-site approval FKs + composite index for D-MS-13 RLS efficiency.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0046_transfer_orders_inter_site.sql` per §9.6 DDL:
   - ALTER TABLE transfer_orders ADD COLUMN from_site_id UUID NULL REFERENCES sites(id).
   - ADD COLUMN to_site_id UUID NULL REFERENCES sites(id).
   - ADD COLUMN transfer_cost NUMERIC(12,2).
   - ADD COLUMN cost_allocation_method TEXT CHECK (cost_allocation_method IN ('sender','receiver','split','none')) DEFAULT 'receiver'.
   - ADD COLUMN expected_arrival_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
   - ADD COLUMN shipped_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
   - ADD COLUMN actual_arrival_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
   - ADD COLUMN from_site_manager_approval_id UUID.
   - ADD COLUMN to_site_manager_approval_id UUID.
   - CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_to_org_site ON transfer_orders(org_id, site_id) — D-MS-13 composite (site_id activated by T-030 ALTER pass; this migration is forward-compatible).
2. Drizzle schema patch `packages/db/src/schema/transfer-orders.ts` (modify existing entry) — add the new cols typed.
3. Zod schema delta — cost_allocation_method enum + CHECK (effective_to >= effective_from-style date ordering for shipped_at vs expected_arrival_at vs actual_arrival_at) at zod refinement layer (DB constraint avoided to allow back-dated corrections).
4. Tests assert: cost_allocation_method CHECK violation on bad value, FK constraint on from_site_id/to_site_id (must reference real site), default 'receiver' on insert.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0046_transfer_orders_inter_site.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/transfer-orders.ts [modify]
- packages/db/src/schema/transfer-orders.zod.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/transfer-orders-inter-site.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0046 applied, when \d transfer_orders inspected, then all 9 new cols are present + cost_allocation_method CHECK constraint present with the 4 allowed values + DEFAULT 'receiver'.
2. Given an insert with cost_allocation_method='unknown', when committed, then DB raises check_violation.
3. Given an insert with from_site_id pointing to a non-existent sites.id, when committed, then DB raises foreign_key_violation.
4. Given idx_to_org_site is checked via pg_indexes, when listed, then a composite index on (org_id, site_id) exists for transfer_orders (D-MS-13).
5. Given an insert with cost_allocation_method omitted, when committed, then the row has cost_allocation_method='receiver' (DEFAULT applied).

## Test strategy
- RED: integration test asserting CHECK + FK + DEFAULT + composite index presence before migration applied.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- transfer-orders-inter-site'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not drop or rename existing transfer_orders columns — 05-WH owns the table; this task is an additive ALTER only.
- Do not add NOT NULL on from_site_id/to_site_id — backward compat: legacy same-site TOs leave both NULL (V-MS-12 same-site skip-IN_TRANSIT path).
- Do not enforce shipped_at < actual_arrival_at at DB level — allow back-dated corrections; validation at app layer in T-009.
- Do not skip CONCURRENTLY on the composite index — large transfer_orders tables would lock writes.
- Do not couple this migration to the site_id ALTER of transfer_orders (T-030 activation owns the site_id column itself).
- RLS on transfer_orders will use app.current_site_id() (never raw GUC) once T-030 applies site-scoped policy via buildSiteScopedPolicySql (T-006).
